authors: Slavin KV, North RB, Deer TR, Staats P, Davis K, Diaz R
title: Tonic and burst spinal cord stimulation waveforms for chronic, intractable pain: randomized controlled trial protocol
study_question: Is burst spinal cord stimulation noninferior to tonic stimulation for chronic pain relief?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic neuropathic pain of trunk/limbs responding to tonic stimulation trial
follow_up_duration: 24 weeks control phase, then 2 years open-label follow-up
outcome_measures: Visual Analog Scale pain scores, Beck Depression Inventory, Oswestry Disability Index, paresthesia presence
inclusion_criteria: Age ≥22, chronic intractable neuropathic pain, baseline VAS ≥60, failed ≥3 treatments, successful tonic trial
answer_to_study_question: Study protocol describes methodology; results pending. Designed to demonstrate burst noninferiority to tonic stimulation.
